content: Had an amazing breakthrough at work today! The client loved our proposal and wants to move forward immediately. I'm feeling so motivated and excited about where my career is heading. Sometimes it's the simple victories that remind you why you chose this path in the first place. Sarah from marketing said she's never seen a client get that excited about a pitch before. I keep replaying the moment when they said 'this is exactly what we've been looking for.' Maybe I'm finally getting the hang of this whole consulting thing.

Spent the weekend with Mom and Dad. It's crazy how being back in my childhood home makes me feel like I'm 16 again. We had a long talk about my career choices over dinner - Dad finally admitted he's proud of me, which honestly made me tear up a little. He said he didn't understand what I did for work, but he could see how passionate I am about it. Mom made my favorite lasagna and we watched old home videos. Sometimes I forget how much I miss them when I'm caught up in city life.

Therapy session was intense today. Dr. Martinez helped me see how I always apologize for taking up space, even in my own life. It's connected to that thing Mom used to say about being 'too much' when I was a kid. I'm 28 and still hearing her voice in my head, but at least now I recognize it. We talked about setting boundaries at work too - I don't have to say yes to every project just because I'm afraid of disappointing people. This is hard work but I can feel myself changing.

Another Zoom meeting that could have been an email. I swear, remote work has some benefits but the meeting culture has gotten completely out of hand. Sarah and I were texting each other during the 'brainstorming session' - she sent me a meme about corporate buzzwords and I almost lost it trying not to laugh. At least we're in this together. Sometimes I miss the office though. There was something about those random hallway conversations that you just can't replicate virtually. Maybe that's just nostalgia talking.

Had a panic attack in the grocery store today. Just completely out of nowhere - one minute I'm comparing pasta sauce prices, the next I can't breathe and everything feels overwhelming. Had to sit in my car for twenty minutes doing the breathing exercises Dr. Martinez taught me. I hate how unpredictable this anxiety is. At least I recognized what was happening and had my coping strategies ready. Called my sister afterward and she reminded me that having tools to deal with it is progress, even if it still sucks when it happens.
